children.heading: Security automation and checklists for Government systems
children.label: c Security automation and checklists for Government systems

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall, as necessary, develop and revise security automation standards, associated reference materials (including protocols), and checklists providing settings and option selections that minimize the security risks associated with each information technology hardware or software system and security tool that is, or is likely to become, widely used within the Federal Government, thereby enabling standardized and interoperable technologies, architectures, and frameworks for continuous monitoring of information security within the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: the security risks associated with the use of the system;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of agencies that use a particular system or security tool;

children.label: C
children.text: the usefulness of the standards, reference materials, or checklists to Federal agencies that are users or potential users of the system;

children.label: D
children.text: the effectiveness of the associated standard, reference material, or checklist in creating or enabling continuous monitoring of information security; or

children.label: E
children.text: such other factors as the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Excluded systems
children.label: 3 Excluded systems
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology may exclude from the application of paragraph (1) any information technology hardware or software system or security tool for which such Director determines that the development of a standard, reference material, or checklist is inappropriate because of the infrequency of use of the system, the obsolescence of the system, or the lack of utility or impracticability of developing a standard, reference material, or checklist for the system.

children.heading: Dissemination of standards and related materials
children.label: 4 Dissemination of standards and related materials
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall ensure that Federal agencies are informed of the availability of any standard, reference material, checklist, or other item developed under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: require any Federal agency to select the specific settings or options recommended by the standard, reference material, or checklist for the system;

children.label: B
children.text: establish conditions or prerequisites for Federal agency procurement or deployment of any such system;

children.label: C
children.text: imply an endorsement of any such system by the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology; or

children.label: D
children.text: preclude any Federal agency from procuring or deploying other information technology hardware or software systems for which no such standard, reference material, or checklist has been developed or identified under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Federal agency information security programs
children.label: d Federal agency information security programs
A
children.text: shall include in that program an explanation of how the agency has considered such checklist in deploying that system; and

children.label: B
children.text: may treat the explanation as if it were a portion of the agency’s annual performance plan properly classified under criteria established by an Executive Order (within the meaning of ).section 1115(d) of title 31

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to any computer hardware or software system for which the National Institute of Standards and Technology does not have responsibility under .section 278g–3(a)(3) of this title
